data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Lose Steam as AI Stocks Face Pressure ahead of Key Jobs Data
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) finished in negative territory on Wednesday as traders sold off AI-related stocks.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • In his first public appearance since the June interest rate decision, Fed Chair Kevin Warsh noted that inflation risks have eased. "Expectations of inflation over the first four weeks of this period have come down, inflation risks have come down," he said at the European Central Bank (ECB) forum in Sintra, Portugal.
  • Rate hike odds trended lower following his comments. The odds of a 25 bps hike at the July 29 Fed meeting on the CME FedWatch tool dropped to 27.3% from 33.1% a day ago. In addition, the odds of two hikes by the September 16 meeting fell to 13.5% from 17.2%.
  • Elsewhere, ADP said private employers added a net 98,000 jobs in June, falling short of the consensus estimate of 118,000 additions. Education and health services accounted for nearly half of the gains at 48,000. On the other hand, natural resources and mining was the only negative sector with a loss of 5,000 jobs.
  • The data provides a preview to June's jobs report, which the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will publish on Thursday at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time. The report includes nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate.
  • AI-related stocks, including Micron (MU), AMD (AMD), and SanDisk (SNDK), faced pressure as investors rotated into beaten-down software names. These include Palantir (PLTR), Salesforce (CRM), and Adobe (ADBE).
  • Meta Platforms (META) popped after announcing a plan to sell excess compute.
  • Robinhood (HOOD) rallied after holding its "The World is Flat" crypto event. The brokerage introduced Robinhood Chain, a public blockchain for tokenized stock products.
  • ServiceNow (NOW) surged after Guggenheim analyst John DiFucci issued a price target of $125 and upgraded NOW to "Buy" from "Neutral."
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: CNN Shakeup as Paula Reid is Expected to Depart, Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) Stock Gains
data.description:
One of the biggest points of concern in the merger between entertainment giant Paramount Skydance (PSKY) and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) is what happens to CNN. Since Paramount already controls CBS News, the idea that CNN may be under the same umbrella is a little too much for some to stomach. Reports note that it may already be too much for one correspondent to stomach as Paula Reid is set to depart before a Paramount era can kick in. Investors took the news oddly well, and gave Paramount shares a nearly 3% boost in the closing minutes of Wednesday's trading.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Paula Reid is currently CNN's chief legal affairs correspondent, reports note, and that Reid was offered the choice to renew her contract, but decided not to do so. Reid departed thanks to "…uncertainty of continuing to work at CNN as it faces a new future under different corporate management."

This, naturally, has raised further questions within CNN, which was already under some strain as it began to consider a possible future under Paramount. Meanwhile, Reid may be planning to move directly to CBS News herself, and likely has at least a few contacts therein to help smooth the process. There is still plenty of uncertainty around the merger process, even as it clears multiple legal hurdles, so continuing to keep an eye on the situation will likely prove worthwhile.

Shutting One Part Down

A part of the merger arrangement many expected to happen, meanwhile, is going official. Paramount plans to shut down its joint venture with Universal (CMCSA), which was said to be part of the required remedy slate from the European Union to get the deal to pass.

The joint venture, known as UIP, got its start all the way back in 1981. These days, it mostly serves as a European distributor, handling Universal films for theatrical release therein. Shutting this operation down should clear the way for EU approval, though there remains concern in Great Britain as well as two state governments in the United States left to address. The British concern, reports noted, was over a perhaps-unduly concentrated number of people handling United Kingdom media.

Is Paramount Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on PSKY stock based on one Buy, four Holds and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 23.21% loss in its share price over the past year, the average PSKY price target of $11.33 per share implies 11.38% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Robinhood (HOOD) Brings Crypto-Style 24/7 Trading to Commodities and Currencies, Stock Surges
data.description:
Investing platform Robinhood (HOOD) is doubling down on 24/7 trading by launching perpetual futures for customers in Europe. These contracts, often called "perps," have no expiration date and allow traders to maintain leveraged positions around the clock. Robinhood said that the new products will be linked to assets such as gold (GLD), silver (SLV), crude oil (USO), and the euro-dollar exchange rate (USD-EUR). In addition, investors can use up to 10x leverage. Interestingly, shares surged on the news.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The move is part of Robinhood's strategy to bring crypto-style market access into mainstream finance. In fact, perpetual futures have been common in crypto for years because digital assets trade nonstop, unlike stocks that often depend on traditional market hours. However, recent global events have made some traders want around-the-clock access to their investments.

For example, during the early weeks of the Iran conflict, traders used decentralized platforms like Hyperliquid to bet on oil and other assets over the weekend while major markets were closed. It's worth noting that Robinhood has already seen strong demand for this type of product. The company launched crypto perpetual futures in Europe last year, and they have since become one of its fastest-growing offerings.

Robinhood Announces New Crypto-Related Products

Separately, Robinhood recently announced new crypto-related products at its London event. These include a decentralized lending product and AI trading accounts for U.S. crypto investors. The company also introduced Crypto Earn, which lets U.S. users lend the USDG stablecoin through a self-custody wallet for an estimated 7% annual yield. Meanwhile, Robinhood said it plans to launch a crypto trading platform in the U.K. soon.

Is HOOD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on HOOD stock based on 16 Buys, three Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average HOOD price target of $104.59 per share implies 3.8% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: S&P 500 Top Stock Gainers Today, 7/1/26
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) finished slightly lower on Wednesday as AI-related stocks faced heavy selling pressure.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Let's break down the sectors behind the performance.

Today's Top-Performing Sector 

Communication services was the top-performing sector on Wednesday. Meta Platforms (META) led the group's gains after the tech conglomerate announced that it would build a new cloud business to sell excess computing. Omnicom (OMC) was also a top performer following reports that it had secured a $512 million global media account deal with Adidas (ADDYY). Finally, Trade Desk (TTD) jumped 5.9%, although the adtech platform is still down by 49% year-to-date.

Several communication services stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Meta Platforms (META)
  • Trade Desk (TTD)
  • Omnicom Group (OMC)
  • News Corp Class B (NWS)
  • News Corp Class A (NWSA)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the information technology sector faced the largest drawdown as investors sold out of AI beneficiaries. These included several stocks with exceptional year-to-date returns, including Corning (GLW), SanDisk (SNDK), and Teradyne (TER). Furthermore, investors may have rotated out of AI stocks and into beaten-down software names, such as Palantir (PLTR), ServiceNow (NOW), and Salesforce (CRM).

Notable information technology stocks trading lower include:

  • Corning (GLW)
  • KLA Corp (KLAC)
  • Teradyne (TER)
  • Lam Research (LRCX)
  • SanDisk (SNDK)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Look Beyond the Noise,' Says Investor as Microsoft Stock Sits at a Crossroads
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) has struggled to find its footing this year, with the stock still down 22% year-to-date. Concerns have piled up, ranging from soaring AI capital spending and heavy reliance on its OpenAI partnership to fears of a looming "SaaSpocalypse."

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The biggest question mark is capital spending. Microsoft expects to pour about $190 billion into capital expenditures this year. While that's broadly in line with some of its hyperscale rivals, a significant portion of that investment is going toward relatively short-lived assets such as GPUs and CPUs, raising questions about how often those assets will need to be refreshed and whether the returns will justify the spending.

Then there's OpenAI. Microsoft's commercial remaining performance obligation (RPO) has climbed to an impressive $627 billion, up 99% from a year ago. However, bears are quick to point out that roughly 45% of that backlog is tied to OpenAI, a company that, despite its explosive growth, has yet to turn a profit.

Another concern making the rounds is the prospect of a "SaaSpocalypse" – the idea that AI agents and capable foundation models could upend the traditional software-as-a-service business model. The bearish argument is that AI could reduce demand for standalone software applications by allowing users to complete many tasks directly through AI assistants.

Yet, one investor, known by the pseudonym Quad 7 Capital, thinks all the fretting is misplaced.

"To us the recent action is an overreaction where short-term sentiment is outweighing the long-term structural dominance of this great American company," states the 5-star investor.

Quad 7 argues that Microsoft is delivering on both the top and bottom lines, posting a "massive earnings beat" in fiscal Q3. Beyond total sales of $82.9 billion (a jump of 18% year-over-year), Microsoft Cloud's revenue rose by 29% to reach $54.5 billion.

When it comes to OpenAI, Quad 7 views Microsoft's decision to remove exclusivity clauses from the partnership as a positive step. The investor also points out that, as a major stakeholder, Microsoft stands to reap a significant windfall if OpenAI eventually goes public.

In addition, Quad 7 appreciates the company's moves vis-a-vis Y Combinator, offering AI startups $150,000 in Azure credits. The investor likes this strategy, believing it will help lock in future revenues by cultivating ongoing relationships between startups and the Microsoft ecosystem.

"Microsoft is paying for the up-front infrastructure costs to guarantee it capture(s) these companies as high-margin, paying customers down the road," adds Quad 7.

Therefore, this year's dip isn't a sign of more trouble on the horizon, according to Quad 7. On the contrary, for those willing to look beyond the noise, this is a great chance to buy a long-term winner before it starts to climb back up again.

 "We think at this valuation, Microsoft sets up as an interesting investment opportunity," concludes Quad 7, who rates MSFT a Buy. (To watch Quad 7's track record, click here)

Wall Street is also feeling good about Microsoft. With 35 Buys and just one Hold, MSFT soars to a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $562.10 implies about 51% upside from current levels. (See MSFT stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Mixed After Strong Quarter Close
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed on Tuesday evening after a strong regular trading session that marked the conclusion of the second quarter and first half of the year. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.03% and 0.02%, respectively, at 6:37 p.m. EDT on June 30, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures were down 0.13%.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

In the regular session, the Nasdaq, the S&P 500, and the Dow rose 1.5%, 0.8%, and 0.3%, respectively, on Tuesday. For the quarter, the S&P climbed 15% and the Nasdaq jumped 21%, their strongest gains since Q2 2020. The Dow rose 13%, just shy of its 15% advance in late 2022. The performance of the Russell 2000 stood out, up 22%, reflecting its best first half since 1991.

Chip and AI stocks have powered much of the market's run, with a record rally adding $2 trillion in value to Micron (MU), Intel (INTC), and AMD (AMD) in Q2.

On the economic front, traders now look ahead to Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's remarks on Wednesday, along with fresh data on jobs and manufacturing.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: FLY and LUNR Stocks Climb After NASA Awards New 2028 Moon Missions
data.description:
NASA said Tuesday it has chosen Firefly Aerospace (FLY), Intuitive Machines (LUNR), and Astrobotic to deliver science payloads to the lunar surface. The project involves four new missions on the Moon in late 2028. These flights are part of NASA's Moon Base Program, which aims to build a long-term human and scientific presence on the Moon. Following the news, FLY rose over 3% in after-hours trading, and LUNR jumped 6.5%.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

FLYT: an alternative to margin or options on FLY

NASA is paying Astrobotic $297.9 million for two landings, Firefly $144.2 million for one landing, and Intuitive Machines $148.3 million for one landing. All missions are part of NASA's Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) program, the main supply line for the Moon Base. Each company will use updated versions of landers that have already flown.

Ryan Stephan, acting director of Moon Base cargo landers, said NASA is building a "proving ground" for future Moon operations. He said faster mission orders and more launch chances will help NASA learn and improve at a quicker pace.

Each 2028 delivery will carry three NASA instruments:

  • SCALPSS: Four cameras that create 3D views of how landing engines kick up lunar dust.
  • LRA: A small laser reflector that helps orbiters and landers track their exact position.
  • LETS: A tiny radiation sensor that measures the strength and type of radiation on the Moon.

Why the NASA Wins Matter for LUNR and FLY

Intuitive Machines and Firefly both stand to gain from NASA's new Moon missions. The fresh CLPS awards give each company more chances to fly proven landers and a stronger role in NASA's long‑term Moon Base plan.

For LUNR, the new 2028 landing adds steady revenue and helps build more flight history for its Nova‑C lander, which boosts its chances of winning more surface missions. For FLY, the Blue Ghost lander gets another major NASA job, giving the company more room to grow its lunar hardware.

Which Is the Best Stock, According to Analysts?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to compare LUNR and FLY stocks. Analysts are most bullish on FLY with a Strong Buy consensus rating. However, LUNR offers the highest 12-month upside potential at 84.2%.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Michael Burry Bets Against Tesla, Nvidia, and Caterpillar as He Warns of an AI Bubble
data.description:
Michael Burry, the famed "Big Short" investor, disclosed on Tuesday that he has opened new short positions against Caterpillar (CAT), Nvidia (NVDA), Applied Materials (AMAT), Tesla (TSLA), and the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX). In a new Substack post, he argued that AI‑linked stocks have become dangerously overvalued.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

Shorting Caterpillar for the First Time

Burry said the most surprising move, even to himself, was shorting Caterpillar at $1,060.98.

"Caterpillar jumped out at me. I have never shorted Caterpillar. It has always done great for me on the long side in the past," he wrote in his Substack post.

Caterpillar has been one of the S&P 500's top stocks in 2026 so far. Shares are up over 86% in the first half of the year as investors treat the heavy equipment maker as a proxy for global AI infrastructure spending. Burry said the stock's valuation has now reached levels he cannot ignore, as the price‑to‑sales ratio is at its highest point in at least 30 years.

Broad Bet Against the AI Trade

Burry's new short book includes:

  • Nvidia stock short at $198.09
  • Applied Materials short at $729.40
  • Tesla short at $416.22
  • iShares Semiconductor ETF short at $642.80

He did not disclose the size of the positions or whether they were built using options.

Burry said he initiated the Tesla short after the stock jumped roughly 10% on Tuesday. Tesla had closed at $379.71 the prior session, meaning he used the rebound to enter the trade rather than shorting into weakness.

Bubble Warning: "Only a Matter of Time Now"

Burry reiterated his long‑running concern that the AI rally has pushed semiconductor valuations to extreme levels. He noted that the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index is trading about 65% above its 200‑day moving average, a level he said was only reached during the dot‑com bubble in 2000.

He also dismissed the latest catalyst, massive chip spending announcements out of South Korea, as a late‑cycle sign rather than a fresh growth driver.

"The proximate cause of today's rally is big spending announced out of Korea. Well, I see that as the beginning of the end. It is only a matter of time now," Burry wrote.

Which Is the Best Stock, According to Analysts?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to compare these stocks.

Analysts are most bullish on AMAT and NVDA, both carrying a Strong Buy consensus rating. Among them, Nvidia offers the highest 12-month upside potential of 54.6%. Meanwhile, TSLA and CAT have Moderate Buy consensus ratings.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: SpaceX Offers Starlink Discounts in Memphis amid Data Center Backlash
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) is offering discounted Starlink internet plans in Memphis, Tennessee, as the aerospace and AI company faces local backlash and legal pressure tied to its nearby AI data centers. Customers who opt in can get Starlink service for half the usual monthly price, with plans normally ranging from $55 to $130 per month.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

In addition, new users will not have to pay upfront hardware costs, and the discount can be shared with friends and family. The move appears to be part of a plan by Elon Musk's company to improve its relationship with the Memphis community. The offer comes after SpaceX acquired xAI earlier this year, bringing the company deeper into the artificial intelligence infrastructure business.

xAI operates major data centers in Memphis and Southaven, Mississippi, including the Colossus site in Tennessee. However, those facilities have drawn criticism from community groups and environmental opponents over pollution concerns. SpaceX powers the Colossus site using gas turbines, and the NAACP has filed a lawsuit accusing the company of violating pollution rules. Interestingly, though, the U.S. Department of Justice came to xAI's defense in that legal fight.

Recycled Wastewater Project in Memphis

At the same time, SpaceX is also moving forward with a recycled wastewater project in Memphis. According to a post from the city's mayor, the company has agreed to resume construction on a wastewater plant that had been delayed after work began in late 2025.

Once completed, the facility is expected to clean wastewater that can be used by both SpaceX's Colossus data centers and the Tennessee Valley Authority, the local electric utility. Overall, the Starlink discount and wastewater project suggest that SpaceX is trying to reduce the local tensions while continuing to grow its AI data center footprint in the region.

Is SPCX Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SPCX stock based on three Buys, three Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average SPCX price target of $222.20 per share implies 30% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Tesla or Rivian: Cantor Picks One EV Stock to Buy Ahead of Upcoming Q2 Delivery Reports
data.description:
Q2 is now on its last legs, and with July about to begin, EV makers are preparing to announce their second-quarter delivery numbers. Both Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN) have struggled to gain traction with investors so far this year, although both seem to be seeing out the quarter with a show of strength, climbing higher as the new week got underway.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

TSLQ: built for a short position on TSLA

On Monday, Tesla recorded its largest single-day gain in more than a year, rising over 8% after it started deploying a significant software update to millions of older vehicles. The rally appeared to be driven by Tesla's rollout of FSD (Full Self-Driving) v14 Lite, a new version of Tesla's autonomous driving software designed for older cars that had not received a significant update in over 14 months.

Can the company keep the momentum when it announces its delivery numbers? That remains to be seen, although Cantor analyst Andres Sheppard thinks deliveries will miss Street expectations.

For Q2, Sheppard "conservatively estimates" vehicle deliveries of 397,414 units, below the sell-side consensus forecast of 408,609. On the other hand, the analyst projects Q2 energy storage deployments of 15.7 GWh, above the consensus estimate of 13.9 GWh.

A delivery miss is unlikely to cause much consternation among investors as the story has now moved well beyond electric cars, a point made by Sheppard. "More importantly," he said, "we continue to expect FY26 to be a transformational year for the company as it transitions into autonomy, AI, robotics, and chips."

Upcoming "potential material catalysts" include robotaxi market expansion, the Tesla Roadster unveil (Q3), and a broader rollout of Full Self-Driving (supervised) in China expected in the second half of 2026. Additional catalysts include the rollout of FSD (supervised) in Europe, pending regulatory approval, as well as the Cybercab rollout, both also expected in 2H26. The Semi Truck is expected to enter production, while Optimus Bot should see initial deliveries in the second half of 2027.

Bottom line, Sheppard assigns Tesla shares an Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating, while his $510 price target points toward 12-month returns of 21%. (To watch Sheppard's track record, click here)

Regarding smaller peer Rivian, the company recently launched its R2 vehicle on June 9, with an initial price of $57,990, ahead of a lower-cost $44,990 variant expected in 2027. Sheppard continues to believe R2 deliveries will support stronger customer demand, driven by its "more competitive" pricing and newly introduced autonomy features. The analyst also sees Rivian's AI-driven, customer-centric autonomous strategy as a potential catalyst that will "materially improve the unit economics and likely capture meaningful autonomy and EV market share in North America (over several years)."

For Q2, Sheppard expects deliveries of 10,234 units, broadly in line with the Visible Alpha consensus forecast of 10,518. He also estimates production of 11,900 vehicles, above the Visible Alpha consensus estimate of 11,132.

All told, Sheppard isn't ready to back Rivian just yet, rating the stock Neutral. Even so, his $19 price target still implies 9.5% upside over the next year.

What does the rest of Wall Street think? Street think? Tesla currently claims a Moderate Buy consensus rating, based on 10 Buy, 15 Hold, and 3 Sell recommendations. However, the average price target of $403.49 suggests a 4% downside from current levels. Rivian also earns a Moderate Buy consensus, supported by 8 Buy, 4 Hold, and 3 Sell ratings. Here, the average price target of $18.25 points to about 5% upside over the coming months. (See TSLA stock forecast or RIVN stock forecast)

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analyst. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel
